title: Why do I still get runc related errors even if I use podman version 3.2.2?
body:
```
podman --runtime $(which crun) ps
ERRO[0000] OCI Runtime runc is in use by a container, but is not available (not in configuration file or not installed) 
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
```
```
podman info --debug
ERRO[0000] OCI Runtime runc is in use by a container, but is not available (not in configuration file or not installed) 
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.21.0
  cgroupControllers: []
  cgroupManager: systemd

  conmon:
    package: /usr/bin/conmon is owned by conmon 1:2.0.29-1

  cpus: 24
  distribution:
    distribution: manjaro
    version: unknown
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: test-HomePC
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0

  linkmode: dynamic

  memTotal: 33579282432

    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.20.1
      commit: 38271d1c8d9641a2cdc70acfa3dcb6996d124b3d
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /etc/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: false
  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: /usr/bin/slirp4netns is owned by slirp4netns 1.1.11-1
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.11
      commit: 368e69ccc074628d17a9bb9a35b8f4b9f74db4c6
      libslirp: 4.6.1
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.5.1
  swapFree: 16788746240
  swapTotal: 16788746240
  uptime: 18m 16.94s
registries:
  docker.io:
    Blocked: false
    Insecure: false
    Location: docker.io
    MirrorByDigestOnly: false
    Mirrors:
    - Insecure: false
      Location: ustc-edu-cn.mirror.aliyuncs.com
    - Insecure: false
      Location: hub-mirror.c.163.com
    - Insecure: false
      Location: registry.docker-cn.com
    Prefix: docker.io
  k8s.gcr.io:
    Blocked: false
    Insecure: false
    Location: registry.cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com/google_containers
    MirrorByDigestOnly: false
    Mirrors: null
    Prefix: k8s.gcr.io
  quay.io:
    Blocked: false
    Insecure: false
    Location: quay.azk8s.cn
    MirrorByDigestOnly: false
    Mirrors: null
    Prefix: quay.io
  search:
  - docker.io
  - k8s.gcr.io

store:
  configFile: /home/test/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 3
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 3
  graphDriverName: vfs
  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /home/test/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus: {}
  imageStore:
    number: 13
  runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers
  volumePath: /home/test/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 3.2.2
  Built: 1625835244
  BuiltTime: Fri Jul  9 20:54:04 2021
  GitCommit: d577c44e359f9f8284b38cf984f939b3020badc3
  GoVersion: go1.16.5
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 3.2.2
```
As far as I know, isn't the default use of crun in podman 3.0?

comments.body: The OCI runtime each container was created with is preserved in its configuration, so it will always use the same one, even if the default changes. You can remove and recreate older containers, or use `podman system migrate --new-runtime crun` to migrate all existing containers to `crun`. Please note that all containers must be stopped for this command to be used.

@mheon In fact, as printed by the ps command, I have deleted all the containers.
Of course I will try the `podman system migrate --new-runtime crun` command after get off work

You're using `podman ps` (which shows only running containers), not `podman ps -a` (which also shows stopped containers). The `podman info` command shows 3 stopped containers, so you did not succeed in removing all of them. A `podman rm -af` should be enough if that's what you want.
Thanks, when I used the `rm -a` command to delete all the containers, the problem seemed to be resolved.
